title: Role representations
description: Schema for shared/representations.csv. A representation is one available way to model an active role. Validation must enforce exactly one selected representation for each active role. The representation layer is the only authoritative place for residual placeholder semantics: when a role is a real coverage obligation but is currently modeled as an explicit placeholder, it is represented with representation_kind = residual_stub rather than tagged as residual at the role, method, or physical-node layer.

representation_id.description: Stable identifier for this representation option.

representation_kind.description: Representation kind. Pathway and technology bundles are direct representations that expose methods. A residual_stub is a direct representation that explicitly models the role as a placeholder until richer pathway or technology methods are authored; it exposes the same kind of method rows as other direct representations but signals at the representation layer that the modeling is intentionally coarse. Role decompositions activate child roles through role_decomposition_edges.csv and do not expose direct methods.

representation_label.description: Human-readable label for this representation option.

description.description: Plain-language description of what this representation covers.

is_default.description: True when this is the default representation for the role. Validation must find exactly one default representation for every role; downstream code derives role defaults from this column rather than from the role row.

notes.description: Implementation or migration notes for this representation.
